name: Hook Model — Trigger / Action / Variable Reward / Investment

fields.definition_origin: Nir Eyal, 'Hooked: How to Build Habit-Forming Products' (Penguin/Portfolio, 2014). Synthesizes BJ Fogg's Behavior Model (B = MAP, Stanford Behavior Design Lab) with Skinner's variable-ratio reinforcement and consumer psychology into a four-phase loop: External Trigger → Action → Variable Reward → Investment, which over repetitions installs an Internal Trigger that replaces the external prompt.
fields.core_mechanism: Onboarding is the user's *first traversal* of the loop and the only chance to bootstrap an internal trigger. (1) External Trigger: install ad, push primer, or onboarding step that promises payoff. (2) Action: the smallest possible behavior that yields perceived value (a tap, a question answered, an avatar chosen). (3) Variable Reward — of the tribe (social proof copy, 'X others like you'), of the hunt (AI plan reveal, mystery-box content), of the self (mastery progress, badge). Unpredictability is what spikes mesolimbic dopamine. (4) Investment: data the user gives (preferences, height/weight, goals, photo, friend graph) — sunk cost that loads the next external trigger and raises switching cost. Onboarding's job is to land all four within the first 5–15 minutes and store an investment artifact (streak, plan, avatar) the user will return for.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Mesolimbic dopamine system, specifically nucleus accumbens activation under variable-ratio reinforcement (Schultz 1997). Berridge's wanting/liking distinction: anticipation of variable reward — not the reward itself — produces the dopamine spike. Same circuitry as gambling and compulsive social-media checking; in onboarding, the AI plan reveal is engineered to fire this anticipation circuit on a single deterministic cue (the loader).
fields.empirical_evidence: Duolingo is the canonical applied case: post-streak architecture deployment, the company reports ~21% lift in current-user retention and 4.5x DAU growth over four years, reaching 113M MAU by Q3 2024 (74M in 2023). Internal Duolingo posts (2024) credit Streak Freeze, Streak Repair, and Friends Quest as highest-leverage retention. Cal AI's 33-step questionnaire→AI-reveal→soft paywall onboarding (Y1 $30M ARR) is a Hook loop compressed into onboarding: trigger (TikTok ad), action (33 micro-questions), variable reward (personalized macro plan reveal), investment (33 answers + birthdate + goals). Mau Baron's Prayer Lock funnel (5min→15min onboarding tripled organic conversion, ~$40K/mo→$150K/mo) operates on the same loop. Counter-evidence: Allcott et al. 2020 Facebook deactivation study (n=2,844) found mood improved when the loop broke; Duolingo's 2023 'Don't let Duo down' notification produced viral backlash. No large-N RCT validates the full Hook loop end-to-end — most evidence is industry case-study.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: D1/D7/D30 retention curves; D7 internal-trigger ratio (sessions opened without push prompt / total sessions); onboarding completion rate; first-investment-event rate (account created, plan generated, avatar saved); time-to-first-action; streak-active rate at D7/D30.
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Duolingo reports ~21% retention lift and 4.5x DAU growth tied to Hook-loop streak architecture. Mau Baron documents 3x organic conversion lift moving from 5min to 15min Hook-installing onboarding. Adapty 2026 onboarding paywall converts 1.35% (highest of any placement) when the Hook investment phase is loaded into the questionnaire. Cal AI's 33-step Hook installation scaled to $30M ARR Y1.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) External Trigger architecture in onboarding: ad-id passed through deep-link to onboarding variant; first 3 screens mirror ad creative semantically. (2) Action minimization: every onboarding step is a single tap or single text input — no multi-field forms. Use big-button conversational pattern. (3) Variable Reward placement: at least one engineered surprise per session — AI plan reveal at step N-1, social-proof punctuation card mid-flow ('5M users like you said the same'), mystery first-day badge unlocked post-paywall. Use Reanimated 3 worklets for 60fps celebration; randomize confetti tier (70/25/5 split for small/medium/jackpot). (4) Investment surface: persist user-supplied data into a visible artifact (streak counter, personalized plan, named avatar) that becomes the user's home-screen anchor; server-store, hydrate on auth so reinstall preserves it. (5) Internal Trigger building: notifications must reference the user's stated goal verbatim ('You said you wanted to lose 10lb — here's today's 1-minute action'). Avoid loss-framed ('Don't lose your streak') copy; prefer gain-framed.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Duolingo Day-0 onboarding: language picker (action) → personalized plan (variable reward) → daily goal slider (investment) → streak-freeze grant on Day-1 (engineered loss-aversion softener). (2) Cal AI onboarding step 22 of 33 — the personalized macro reveal screen with theatrical 4-7s loader animating 'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you' is the engineered Variable Reward. Step 33 = the soft paywall, gated by the Investment phase the user just completed. (3) Phantom Wallet's 'spin the wheel' on first wallet connection — visceral pseudo-random Variable Reward in fintech. (4) Mau Baron's Prayer Lock: 15-min onboarding ends with AI-personalized prayer reveal (Variable Reward of the self) → paywall — the prayer is the Investment artifact, returned-for tomorrow. (5) Snapchat Snapstreak: fire emoji + day count next to friend's name; Eyal cites this as canonical social Investment. (6) Headspace daily-goal picker → first session → streak start.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile is the native habitat of Hook loops — push notifications, lock-screen Live Activities (iOS 16.1+) and Android Live Updates, haptic-driven reward feedback, biometric re-entry (Face ID) make Triggers cheaper and Actions easier than web. Web habit formation is weaker; depends on tab-pinning, browser notifications (mostly dismissed), and email Triggers. Onboarding-specific: mobile gets the OS push permission as a built-in Trigger architecture; web has none equivalent. Web equivalent: email lifecycle drips + browser push (low opt-in) + bookmarklet/desktop-app prompts.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Aligned with both 2025-2026 platform languages. Material 3 Expressive (I/O 2025, 18k-participant study) treats motion springs and shape-morphing celebrations as first-class primitives — the Variable Reward delivery surface. Apple Liquid Glass (WWDC25) `.glassEffect(.interactive)` shimmer on tap is itself a micro-Variable-Reward channel. Both invite the celebration moment Hook depends on.
fields.category_fit: Strongest for: wellness/habit (Duolingo, Calm, Headspace, Cal AI, Flo), AI-wrapper (Cal AI, Prayer Lock), social (Snapchat, Instagram), gaming. Acceptable: fitness, edtech, journaling. Weakest: fintech-utility (a Hook loop in a banking app reads as predatory; use trust-building patterns instead), B2B productivity (Linear/Notion users don't want streak guilt). DO NOT use Hook-loop loss aversion in apps targeting under-18 (AADC/COPPA risk).
fields.tooling_options: RevenueCat (subscription Investment surface), Adapty (paywall + onboarding state), Plotline (onboarding flow + push primer), OneSignal (push triggers), PostHog (Variable Reward A/B test), Reanimated 3 + react-native-skia (celebration), Lottie/Rive (mascot animation). Avoid: bare React Native Animated for celebrations (drops frames on low-end Android).
fields.ai_dependency: optional: Hook works without AI; but the strongest 2024-2026 implementations (Cal AI, Prayer Lock, Headspace AI Stories) use an LLM to generate the Variable Reward (personalized plan/prayer/story). When AI is used, requires GPT-4-class reasoning for plan generation (~1-3s latency) plus offline-fallback static reveal.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude to wire all four phases as discrete, testable components. Example fragment 1: 'Build an onboarding finalizer screen with the four-phase Hook loop. Trigger: arrived from step 32. Action: single primary button \"Reveal my plan\" (≥48dp, full-width, brand color, sensoryFeedback .impact on press). Variable Reward: 4-7 second theatrical loader with Reanimated 3 morphing copy stack (\"Analyzing your responses\" → \"Cross-referencing 2.4M users\" → \"Building your plan\"), then full-bleed reveal of personalized plan card with 70/25/5 confetti tier roll. Investment: persist plan_id to backend AND to MMKV; show plan_id at top of every subsequent screen as the user's anchor.' Example fragment 2: 'Audit every onboarding screen for which Hook phase it serves. Output a table: step → phase → artifact persisted. Forbid steps that serve no phase.'
fields.stitch_application: Stitch must be told the loop is the artifact, not the screen. Example fragment 1: 'Generate onboarding sequence of 6 frames showing the Hook loop. Frame 1 (Trigger): full-bleed promise screen mirroring TikTok ad creative, 64pt Cabinet Grotesk display headline. Frame 2-4 (Action): conversational question screens, single question, 4 big-button options (96dp tall), thumb-zone bottom-anchored CTA. Frame 5 (Variable Reward): personalized plan reveal screen — full-bleed, plan name in 56pt rounded display type, brand-color badge, 3 stat cards revealed in stagger (200ms each), confetti motif unique to brand (NOT generic Lottie). Frame 6 (Investment): home dashboard showing plan name + Day 1 of 30 streak counter as the dominant visual element. Forbid Inter font; use Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen typically: (1) makes the Variable Reward deterministic (same confetti every time — fixed-ratio schedule that extinguishes engagement); (2) skips the Investment artifact and treats onboarding as a checklist with no persistent home-screen anchor; (3) defaults to loss-framed streak copy ('Don't lose your streak'); (4) reaches for indigo-500 + purple-blue gradient defaults that read as AI slop and break brand investment. Counter-prompt: 'Implement three randomized Variable Reward tiers (small/medium/jackpot, weighted 70/25/5) with worklet-based randomization. Persist a named Investment artifact (plan_id, streak_id, named_avatar) and surface it on every post-onboarding screen. Forbid loss-framed notification copy. Replace indigo-500/purple-blue gradient with brand palette explicitly listed at top of file.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Punitive loss aversion ('Don't let Duo down' incident, 2023, generated viral backlash and reduced retention in receiving cohort). (2) Deterministic rewards that train tolerance — same confetti every win is a fixed-ratio schedule. (3) Manufactured social pressure with no off-ramp (Snapstreak with no 'pause' option). (4) Variable rewards on top of competence systems (slot-machine on top of skill tree) — undermines intrinsic motivation per Murayama et al. 2010. (5) Triggers exploiting negative internal triggers without satisfying the user's stated goal. Cross-references the Six-Antipattern Audit's 'Premature Authentication' (Hook can't form if user is gated before value is felt) and 'Wasted Loading Screens' (the loader IS the Variable Reward — never blank).
fields.regulatory_risk: High: EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026 draft) explicitly names addictive design and dopamine loops; streaks, variable rewards, FOMO notifications, infinite scroll. UK AADC and California AADC prohibit nudge techniques on minors. COPPA (under-13, 2025 amendments) and GDPR Article 8 (under-16) require verifiable parental consent for behavioral profiling, which Hook entails. BEUC SHEIN complaint (June 2025) signals regulator appetite. Disney $10M FTC settlement (Dec 2025) sets enforcement tone. Audit any Hook mechanic for minors before EU launch.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: iOS App Store Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization in onboarding constrains how much Investment data can be collected upfront; collect only data tied to declared core function. Apple Guideline 3.1.2 (effective 2026-01) bans toggle paywalls — the Hook Investment-to-Paywall handoff must use side-by-side plan selection or transparent timeline. Google Play Data Safety requires declaration of behavioral profiling. Target API 35 (Android, deadline 2025-08-31) requires runtime-permission discipline.
fields.accessibility_implications: Variable-reward animations must respect prefers-reduced-motion (replace with crossfade). Celebrations must be skippable and not gate progress (WCAG 2.2 §2.3.3 Animation). Streak loss can create anxiety for users with OCD, ADHD, depression — provide explicit 'pause streak' affordance. Push triggers must support VoiceOver/TalkBack; emoji-only push copy is screen-reader-hostile. Touch targets ≥24×24px (WCAG 2.2 §2.5.8). Sound-on celebrations must respect mute.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Habit formation is amoral — same mechanic powers Duolingo (genuine learning) and TikTok (attention extraction). Use Eyal's own Manipulation Matrix as ethical filter. (2) Performance: 60-120fps reward animations drain battery; use Reanimated worklets, not JS-thread Animated. (3) Honest-signal test: a habit that survives a 7-day uninstall is intrinsic; one that collapses is extrinsic — measure both. (4) Offline fallback for AI-generated Variable Rewards mandatory; cache last-known plan locally. (5) Hook loops compound — over-installing creates burnout and uninstall.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Underlying dopamine mechanism is species-universal. Cultural expression varies: collectivist markets (CN/JP/KR) respond more strongly to Variable Rewards 'of the tribe' (clan systems, gifting, group quests — see WeChat hongbao, Honor of Kings) than individual streak counters. Western individualist 'YOUR streak' under-performs vs. 'OUR clan streak' in those markets. RTL (AR) requires mirrored streak/progress UI. 'Don't let Duo down' guilt copy reads aggressive in JP/DE, motivating in some LATAM markets — localize tone, not just words.
fields.prescribed_action: Wire the four Hook phases as discrete, persisted components in onboarding: place at least one engineered Variable Reward (theatrical loader + AI plan reveal) at step N-1, persist a named Investment artifact (plan_id, named avatar, or streak) before the paywall, and forbid loss-framed notification copy. Audit every onboarding screen against the four phases and remove any screen that serves none.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: The questionnaire IS the Action + Investment phase. Sequence questions to compound investment (low-friction → identity → goals → painpoint), each answered question is a sunk-cost deposit feeding the Variable Reward (AI plan reveal) at step N-1.
fields.aha_relevance: The Variable Reward phase is the engineered Aha moment. In a properly wired Hook onboarding, Aha is created BY the Variable Reward (AI plan reveal) at step N-1 — before the user has touched the core feature. This is what allows long onboarding (Cal AI 33 steps) to outperform short onboarding on conversion: the Aha is front-loaded into the loop.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Strongest with Magician (transformation language, AI reveal as the spell) and Hero (mastery progression). Compatible with Jester (playful Variable Rewards) and Lover (relatedness via tribe rewards). Conflicts with Caregiver and Sage archetypes when implemented with loss-framed loss-aversion — soften copy for these voices. Pairs with .agent/research/emotional-design/results/Jungian_Brand_Archetypes.json.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Wasted Loading Screens (the loader IS the Variable Reward — never blank); Premature Authentication (Hook can't form if user is gated before value is felt); Generic Celebration (mismatched archetype voice kills the Variable Reward).
sources: [How Duolingo Gamified Monthly Active Users — The PM Repo](https://www.thepmrepo.com/articles/how-duolingo-gamified-monthly-active-users-lessons-in-habit-formation)
[Goodwin — DFA From Dark Patterns to Fair Play (Nov 2025)](https://www.goodwinlaw.com/en/insights/publications/2025/11/alerts-practices-antc-from-dark-patterns-to-fair-play)
